prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Warhammer Fantasy / The Old World inspired grimdark world map for the terrain type diverse_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: a vast stormy old-world sea filled with many grim island groups and microcontinents, empire harbor city-states, fortress islands, dwarf mountain isles, Chaos-tainted dark forests, northern frozen islands, blighted marsh chains, plague reefs, ruined citadels, pirate havens, witch-hunter outposts and blasted corrupted wastelands.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated dark fantasy atlas, old world parchment cartography, inked relief terrain, oppressive threatening sky, corruption scars, grim and perilous campaign map atmosphere.
Composition/framing: tall portrait world map with unevenly distributed island clusters; include large islands, tiny islets, crescent island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, fragmented cliffs and microcontinents with no grid-like spacing; include compass rose, severe map border, sea routes, fortress icons and corruption symbols without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must vary strongly in size and shape, with realistic complex coastlines, broken shores, curved bays, peninsulas, fjords, deltas, marshy estuaries, volcanic ridges and storm-wrecked coasts; show many climates and biomes across the archipelago including frozen wastes, temperate empire lands, dark forests, mountains, swamps, moors, plague marshes and chaos-blasted badlands.
Avoid: evenly spaced islands, simple round islands, cheerful tropical palette, modern satellite image, board-game UI, readable labels, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
